items.hook: Emperor Gia Long conquers Hanoi and unified Viet Nam, which had experienced centuries of feudal warfare.
items.body: Gia Long, born Nguyễn Phúc Ánh (阮福暎) or Nguyễn Ánh (阮暎), was the founding emperor of the Nguyễn dynasty, the last dynasty of Vietnam, which would rule the unified territories that constitute modern-day Vietnam until 1945. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Magic rituals took place in an Australian cave for 25,000 years
items.body: Preserved plant cells show that grasses were brought into Cloggs cave in Australia and burnt over many millennia, revealing the ancient origins of rituals practised by the GunaiKurnai people

items.hook: Martial law in Poland is officially revoked.
items.body: Martial law in Poland existed between 13 December 1981 and 22 July 1983. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Hurling Team of the Century was chosen as part of the Gaelic Athletic Association's centenary year celebrations in 1984 to comprise, as a fifteen-member side divided as one goalkeeper, three full-backs, three half-backs, two midfielders, three half-forwards and three full-forwards, the best hurling players of the first one hundred years of the Gaelic Athletic Association.
items.body: The players on the team were nominated by Sunday Independent readers and were selected by a panel of experts and former players. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Jean Charles de Menezes is killed by police as the hunt begins for the London Bombers responsible for the 7 July 2005 London bombings and the 21 July 2005 London bombings.
items.body: Jean Charles da Silva de Menezes was a Brazilian man fatally shot and wrongfully killed by the Metropolitan Police Service at Stockwell Station of the London Underground, after being mistakenly identified as one of the fugitives from the previous day's failed bombing attempts. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Pan Am Flight 816 crashes after takeoff from Faa'a International Airport in Papeete, French Polynesia, killing 78.
items.body: Pan Am Flight 816 was an international flight from Auckland, New Zealand, to San Francisco, California, via Tahiti, French Polynesia, and Los Angeles, California. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Forgery and Counterforgery: The Use of Literary Deceit in Early Christian Polemics is a 2012 scholarly monograph by American New Testament historian Bart D.
items.body: Published by Oxford University Press, the volume surveys first- through fourth-century Christian writings that claim false authorship and evaluates how contemporaries received them. One of those things you can't un-know.
